name: Wagyu Beef Tartare Spheres with Smoked Bone Marrow Foam and Pickled Shallot Crisp
chef: Thomas Keller (with Heston Blumenthal molecular influences)
description: Delicate spherified A5 Wagyu beef tartare spheres that burst with hand-chopped texture, paired with airy smoked bone marrow foam and crispy pickled shallot elements. A refined, multi-sensory Michelin-level starter showcasing pure beef flavor with surprise and elegance.
keywords: wagyu, beef tartare, spherification, molecular gastronomy, bone marrow, fine dining, starter

winePairings.name: Champagne (Blanc de Blancs)
winePairings.notes: Crisp bubbles and minerality cut through the richness while enhancing the delicate spheres.

winePairings.name: Chablis Grand Cru
winePairings.notes: Pure Chardonnay with high acidity balances the umami of marrow and bright pickled shallot.

ingredients.item: A5 Wagyu beef tenderloin or ribeye

steps.instruction: Chill Wagyu and hand-chop finely. Mix with shallot, capers, cornichons, mustard, Worcestershire, egg yolk, olive oil, salt, and pepper. Refrigerate.
Pickle shallot slices in rice vinegar, sugar, and salt mixture. Dehydrate or quick-fry until crisp.
Prepare smoked bone marrow foam: Roast marrow, blend with reduced stock and cream, cold-smoke, then aerate with siphon or Pacojet.
Create spherification bath with sodium alginate. Mix calcium lactate into tartare. Form and set spheres in the bath, rinse, and hold in cold water.
Plate with Kunz spoon: balsamic smear, tartare spheres, marrow foam quenelle, shallot crisps, micro herbs, and gold leaf if desired.
